_description: v7.8 framework-v7-8-branch-isolation Q3 (T3): allowlist of glob patterns exempt from BRANCH_ISOLATION_VIOLATION when those paths are the ONLY staged files. Used by the gate to allow legitimate-on-main work (CLAUDE.md edits during freeze, security patches, framework-version-bump commits) without forcing worktree creation. Each entry has a rationale + optional expiry. Empty patterns array = no exemptions = strict mode. Per integration-spec.md §3.3 + PRD §3.1.

patterns.reason: Project-rules file. Edits to CLAUDE.md during framework freezes or for typo fixes/section additions are legitimate-on-main work. The branch-isolation gate would otherwise auto-create a worktree for every CLAUDE.md edit, which is overhead.
DEV-only framework guide. Version-bump edits (e.g. v7.7 → v7.8 → v7.9 timeline) are legitimate-on-main; mostly maintained alongside CLAUDE.md updates as a paired doc-edit set.
Integrity-cycle documentation. Updated when new check codes ship; co-edited with the gate implementations.

_notes: Exemption applies ONLY when the staged commit's file list is a SUBSET of the patterns array. A commit that touches CLAUDE.md AND a feature file is NOT exempt — full isolation enforcement applies.
expires_at: null means the exemption is permanent. Date-bounded exemptions (e.g. for a specific freeze window) should set expires_at to the ISO 8601 date past which the exemption no longer applies.

Block B's BRANCH_ISOLATION_VIOLATION predicate (T6) reads this file via the existing flock_writer.py shared-state pattern. Default behavior: if file doesn't exist or fails to parse, treat as empty patterns (strict mode).
v7.9 promotion may add programmatic exemption-rotation review: any pattern with expires_at set is auto-deleted on its expiry date, and any pattern without expires_at is reviewed annually.
